title: Evaluate functions from their graph
description: Evaluate functions for specific inputs given the graph of the function.
topic_path: Module 5: Examples of functions from geometry

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the output, $y$, when the input, $x$, is $2$.**


$y=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]


[[☃ image 1]]
hints: We should look for the point on the graph whose $x$-coordinate is $2$.

The point on the graph whose $x$-coordinate is $2$ is the point $(2,-2)$.

 

[[☃ image 1]]
When the input is $2$, the output is $-2$.

options.alt: A graph made up of connected line segments. From left to right the graph passes through the points (negative 10, 2), (negative 9, 1), (negative 8, 1), (negative 7, 0), (negative 6, 1), (negative 5, 2), (negative 4, 1), (negative 3, 3), (negative 2, 1), (negative 1, 0), (0, 0), (1, negative 1), (2, negative 2), (3, negative 1), (4, 0), (5, 1), (6, 0), (7, 1), (8, 0), (9, negative 1), and (10, negative 2).
